title: About
text:
I grew up in the Greater Boston area and am currently a student at Lawrence University, where I study quantitative economics and history, with a minor in statistics and data science.
Outside the classroom, I founded the Lawrence University Investment Club and have served as both Vice President and President, where I focus on risk management and help peers build rigorous stock proposals. I am also a member of the Lawrence University hockey team and a graduate of Northfield Mount Hermon School.
My interests sit at the intersection of data, economics, and history. I am drawn to social questions and to understanding how larger forces shape individual outcomes.

title: Joseph (Jeb) Dee
text:
I grew up in the Greater Boston area. It taught me early the value of competition and discipline. At Lawrence University, I study quantitative economics and history, with a minor in statistics and data science. As vice president of the student investment fund, I work on risk management and help fellow students build stock proposals that can stand up to scrutiny. It has taught me patience, humility, and empathy.
I am a graduate of Northfield Mount Hermon School in Massachusetts and a member of the Lawrence University hockey team. Those experiences have shaped the way I think: stay composed, do the work, and trust preparation when the moment comes. I am drawn to difficult questions, to making sense of moving parts, and to understanding how larger forces shape individual lives. That interest is what ties together my work in economics, history, and data.

Email · LinkedIn · GitHub

title: Projects
text:
CompMod — Comparable Company Analysis R Package
R Package · Finance & Data Science
Built an R package for comparable company analysis. Reads valuation data from Excel, computes trading multiples (EV/Revenue, EV/EBITDA, P/E), and summarizes peer group statistics across a peer group of firms.
View on GitHub →


SQL Database Analysis
Team Project · Data Science
Used SQL to query and analyze structured datasets, joining across multiple tables to extract and summarize key insights from large data sources.
View on GitHub →


Text Analysis
Team Project · Data Science
Applied NLP techniques including tokenization and sentiment analysis to real-world text data, visualizing patterns and drawing meaningful conclusions.
View on GitHub →


“Fading Before the Eye” — Harrison Symposium Paper
Research Paper · History · Presented at the Harrison Symposium, 2026
Presented at Lawrence University’s Harrison Symposium. This paper examines the North, Central and South American Exposition of 1885–1886 in New Orleans, arguing that the promise of the New South never reached the city. Drawing on railroad promotional literature, newspaper archives, and historical scholarship, the paper shows how the Illinois Central Railroad used Civil War memory and erased racial history to market New Orleans to northern investors — and why it failed.
Download Paper →


“Installment and Escape Plans” — History Podcast Script
Podcast Script · History 246
Written to accompany an actual podcast episode, this script tells the story of Harry Faulkner Irvine — a traveling cash register salesman convicted of embezzlement in London in 1904. Through his story, the piece explores how immigration, labor, and class collided in the transatlantic commercial world of the early twentieth century. Five days after his release from prison, Irvine boarded the Carpathia — the same ship that would later rescue Titanic survivors.
Download Script →


Personal Website
Quarto Website · Data Science
Designed and built a personal portfolio website using Quarto and R. The site showcases academic work, data science projects, and professional background. Built with a custom dark navy theme, styled entirely in CSS, and deployed through GitHub Pages. The project involved learning version control, file management, and web publishing through a reproducible workflow.
View on GitHub →

title: Projects
section: CompMod — Comparable Company Analysis R Package
text:
CompMod — Comparable Company Analysis R Package
Data Science / Finance — ADC 405, Spring 2026
Built a fully documented R package for comparable company (comps) analysis — a core method in equity valuation. The package reads valuation data from Excel, computes key trading multiples (EV/Revenue, EV/EBITDA, P/E), and summarizes peer group statistics including mean, median, min, and max. Designed to be installable directly from GitHub and usable by anyone conducting stock analysis.
View on GitHub

title: Projects
text:
SQL Database Analysis
Data Science — ADC 405, Spring 2026
Used SQL to query, filter, and analyze structured datasets as part of a team project. Work involved writing relational queries to extract meaningful insights from large data tables, joining across multiple tables, and summarizing findings.
View on GitHub

title: Projects

Data Science — ADC 405, Spring 2026

title: Activities
text: At Lawrence University I founded the Investment Club and have served as both Vice President and President. The work of the club centers on developing rigorous investment research and building stock proposals that can withstand serious scrutiny. Managing risk and guiding peers through the discipline of financial analysis has been among the most formative experiences of my undergraduate years. The club has pushed me to think carefully about evidence, to argue precisely, and to understand how broader economic forces shape individual decisions.

title: Activities
text:
Hockey
Hockey has been the defining thread of my life. Growing up in the Greater Boston area the sport became central to how I understand competition and preparation. Playing junior hockey allowed me to travel across the country competing at a high level in a sport I love. Those years on the road introduced me to players and communities from every corner of the nation and gave me an understanding of competition and perseverance that no classroom could replicate.
The experience of junior hockey was inseparable from the long hours spent in the car between rinks and cities. Those drives became their own kind of education. My mother made all of it possible. She spent countless hours in cold arenas and on long roads making sure I could pursue the game at the level I wanted to. Her commitment to my development as a player and as a person is something I carry with me. I now compete as a member of the Lawrence University hockey team. Hockey rewards composure under pressure and punishes carelessness and those lessons have shaped the way I approach academic work and professional challenges alike.
View my Elite Prospects profile →

title: Activities
text:
Personal
Golf is one of my favorite ways to spend time away from work and study. I grew up playing with my father and those rounds remain some of the most enjoyable I can point to. The game is patient and unforgiving in equal measure. It asks you to compete honestly against yourself and to accept the results of your own decisions.
There is something that cannot quite be explained about the relationship between a boy and his dog. Ulysses is my chocolate Labrador and from the moment he entered my life he became something more than a pet. He is a presence. He has a way of meeting you exactly where you are without asking anything of you in return. The bond between a person and a dog is one of the oldest relationships in human history and I think it endures because it is uncomplicated by the expectations and conditions that define so many other relationships.
